content: We can reframe intrinsic curiosity bonuses from ICM/RND curious RL agents as *dynamic, adaptive filters* that only prioritize failed trajectories for self-supervised world model fine-tuning, instead of weighting all novel exploration equally. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where curiosity only rewards exploration that corrects the agent's flawed world model predictions from prior failed attempts, cutting down on redundant uninformative exploration common to standard ICM/RND.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
